level_signals: 5+ years of experience in revenue enablement and/or partner enablement
Compensation range $120,000–$155,000 base, consistent with Senior IC
Expected to own end-to-end programs independently across multiple workstreams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: AI fluency is required. You already use AI productivity tools daily to draft, analyze, and accelerate your work.
Own end-to-end enablement strategy and execution for Channel, Alliances, and GSI partners, including program design, stakeholder alignment, content development, and technical enablement coordination
Own the ongoing health and evolution of the GTM new hire onboarding program, ensuring content stays current as the platform, processes, and positioning evolve
Maintain sales playbooks as living documents on a regular update cadence, reflecting changes in process, org structure, product capabilities, and competitive positioning
Leverage AI tools daily to accelerate content creation, streamline program management, and multiply individual output
Build lightweight automations, workflows, or internal tools using AI coding tools and our existing toolstack to solve for enablement needs and embed solutions

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in revenue enablement and/or partner enablement, ideally in B2B SaaS or conversational AI / AI-native organizations
Direct experience onboarding and enabling external channel and alliance partners, not just internal reps, with a clear understanding of how these three motions differ
Strong program management skills with a track record of holding internal and external stakeholders accountable while keeping multiple workstreams on track independently
Experience with LMS/enablement platforms (LetterAI, or similar) for building and managing internal learning, and PRMs for partner enablement program

Familiarity with sales methodology enablement (specifically with ability to translate for partners) and deal qualification frameworks (Force Management's Command of the Message, MEDDPPICC, or similar)
A systems-thinking approach: builds once, scales across audiences, retires what's outdated
Data orientation with experience tracking onboarding completion, partner readiness metrics, playbook adoption, and using that data to iterate programs proactively
Comfort with technical products and the ability to translate complex platform capabilities into partner-ready and rep-ready materials
Preferred: experience with Claude Code or similar AI coding tools via terminal to build lightweight apps or automations that solve enablement and operational needs

scope.surface: Partner and GTM enablement programs — Channel, Alliances, GSI partners and internal GTM reps
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, voice AI / conversational AI platform sold through direct and channel/alliance/GSI motions
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end enablement strategy and execution for Channel, Alliances, and GSI partners
Navigate multiple partner program types with distinct enablement needs
Wrangle cross-functional stakeholders to extract knowledge and inputs for partner-ready materials
Partner with Channel & Alliances team to align enablement with partner go-to-market strategies
Own the GTM new hire onboarding program and its ongoing evolution
Manage onboarding logistics end to end including facilitation and performance tracking
Maintain sales playbooks as living documents on a regular update cadence
Identify gaps in playbook coverage as new functions or segments are added
Leverage AI tools daily to accelerate content creation and streamline program management
Build lightweight automations, workflows, or internal tools using AI coding tools

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits including 100% single medical, dental & vision, 401k, flexible PTO, parental leave, fertility programmes
Clear ownership scope — programs, onboarding, playbooks, partner enablement
WFH allowance provided (noise-cancelling headphones or desk chair)
Annual L&D allowance signals investment in employee growth
Role sits at intersection of two high-impact areas (partner + internal enablement) offering broad influence
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite; no city listed
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
AI fluency listed as 'required' in body but the overall requirements section is framed with 'We'd love it if you have' — creates ambiguity about what is truly hard vs. soft
Salary note says range is 'a guide, not a guarantee' which adds compensation uncertainty
JD uses 'LetterAI' as an LMS example — a relatively niche/emerging tool that may indicate an early-stage or rapidly-changing toolstack

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of ownership; not explicitly stated)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'depending on ... location' language in JD)
ambiguities: AI fluency is stated as 'required' in the body of the JD under 'AI-Powered Enablement Delivery', yet all requirements including this are introduced with 'We'd love it if you have:' — treated as hard based on the explicit 'required' language within the section.
Location is entirely unstated — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite indication. Marked as 'unclear'.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
